semanticCore: Success, reconciling, matching, agreement, harmony, or luck
quranicRelevance: God bringing reconciliation (Tawfeeq - 'yuwaffiqi Allahu'), and matching/fitting reward ('jaza-an wifagan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Success, divine assistance, reconciliation, or guidance to right action
Fitting, matching, harmonious, appropriate, or exact

figurativeOrRareUsages: جَزَاءً وَفَاقًا (78:26): An appropriate/exact fitting recompense (exact justice of Hell punishments).
summaryEn: The root denotes harmony and fitting match, applied to marital dispute reconciliation (Tawfeeq), and the exact, fitting reward (Wifaq) of judgment.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على التوافق والمطابقة والصلح، واصفاً صلح الزوجين بتوفيق إلهي، والجزاء العادل لذنوب الكفار دون زيادة (جزاءً وفاقاً).
rawText: أَوْفَقَ أَمْرَهُ 4 He found his affair, or case, agreeable with his wish, or desire. (TA.) إِتِّفَاقِىٌّ Casual. اِتَّفَقَ 8 It happened; chanced. So used in the K, art. لفت, and in many other works. === اِنَّفَقَ مَعَهُ عَلَى أَمْرٍ: see 3. تَوْفِيقٌ A certain legal document: a pleading. See مَحْضَرٌ. وَافَقَهُ 3 He, or it, was conformable, or did conformably, to him, or it; was as he, or it, was, or did as he, or it, did: coincided with him, or it: it suited him, or it: it matched it; tallied with it. === وَافَقَهُ عَلَى أَمْرٍ i. q. مَعَهُ 🡻اِتَّفَقَ عَلَيْهِ, He agreed with him, or was of one mind or opinion with him, upon, or respecting, a thing, or an affair. (TA.) === [وَافَقَهُ is best rendered He agreed, consented, accorded, or was of one mind or opinion, with him: and he complied with him, or it (see طَاوَعَهُ): and he coincided with him, suited him, or it; it matched it, &c.] === وَافَقَ العَذَابُ الذَّنْبَ [The punishment agreed, or corresponded with the sin, crime, or offence]. (TA.) === وَافَقَ It was suitable, or convenient. === See مُشَاكَلَةٌ in art. شكل, conj. 3. === وَافَقَهُ He encountered him; syn. with صَادَفَهُ, q. v. وَفِقَ أَمْرُهُ 1 His affair, or case, was right, agreeable with what was wished, or desired. (A, TA.) وَفَّقَ أَمْرَهُ 2 [He accomodated, adapted, or disposed, his affair to its object; directed it to a right issue;] prospered it. (TK.) === وَفَّقَهُ لِأَمْرٍ He disposed him, or adapted him, to a thing; he disposed him, or made him fit, for a thing. === وَفَّقَهُ ٱللّٰهُ God accommodated him, adapted him, or disposed him, or directed him, to the right course; syn. سَدَّدَهُ: (Msb:) God made him to take, or follow, a right way, course, or direction, [in an affair]: or directed him by inspiration to that which was good, or to prosperity. (TA.) === وَفَّقَهُ لِلسَّدَادِ [He accommodated, adapted, disposed, or directed him, to that which was right]. (K, art. سد.) === وَفَّقَ بين الشَّيْئَيْنِ He effected an agreement, a harmony, a reconciliation, an accommodation, or an adjustment, between the two things. (MA.) And وَفَّقْتُ بَيْنَ القَوْمِ [I effected an agreement, a harmony, &c., between the people, or party; made peace between them]. (Msb, in art. صلح; &c.)
